mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which directly boosts corporate earnings expectations and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which triggers short-term momentum buying in equity markets over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns during monetary tightening phases, or the signal reverses direction when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product confirms macroeconomic expansion, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns across multiple business cycles, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation readings exceeding 0.4 percent trigger expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 55% of instances when CPIAUCSL exceeds the threshold, or the signal loses predictive power when core inflation is stable.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and declines in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to predict positive returns when wage growth stagnates, or the interaction effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production signals weakening manufacturing demand and potential recession risks, prompting defensive portfolio positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to predict negative 5-day returns during periods of accommodative monetary policy, or the signal shows positive returns in > 50% of triggered cases.
